title: phylica axillaris var. axillaris
intro: Phylica axillaris var. axillaris, also known as the Cape beech or Rock beech, is a small, woody shrub native to South Africa, characterized by its glossy, dark green leaves and white, star-shaped flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Phylica axillaris var. axillaris include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
